title: Quantum Algorithms for Feature Selection in Machine Learning: Addressing NP-Completeness with Quantum Computing
abstract: In the realm of machine learning, feature selection is a critical task for improving model efficiency and interpretability. The FOCUS algorithm, as demonstrated by Almuallim and Dietterich, searches for the smallest possible input feature set, but its superpolynomial runtime highlights the NP-completeness of this problem. This paper explores the potential of quantum computing to address this computational bottleneck. We propose a quantum algorithm that leverages quantum parallelism and entanglement to efficiently solve the MIN-FEATURES problem, which is shown to be NP-complete by reducing VERTEX-COVER to MIN-FEATURES. Our quantum approach is compared against classical methods, including the FOCUS algorithm and other feature selection techniques. Experimental simulations on quantum computers demonstrate significant speedup, suggesting that quantum computing can revolutionize feature selection in machine learning. We also discuss the implications of our findings for broader applications in knowledge discovery and pattern recognition.
